steps.title: The Three Laws
steps.text: Every strong opening rests on three pillars: (1) control the center, (2) develop your pieces quickly, (3) get your king safe. Let's practice all three.

steps.prompt: Start by pushing a central pawn. Play e4 or d4.
steps.hint: Central pawns claim space and open lines for your pieces.

steps.title: Now Develop
steps.text: Pawns first — then pieces. Knights before bishops. Knights out toward the center (f3, c3) attack four central squares each.

steps.prompt: Develop a knight toward the center.
steps.hint: Nf3 and Nc3 both hit four central squares.

steps.title: Bishops Next
steps.text: With both knights out, develop bishops to active squares. Bc4 eyes the weak f7 square — the most vulnerable point in Black's camp in the opening.

steps.prompt: Develop the light-squared bishop to an active square.
steps.hint: Bc4 is the classical choice — straight at f7.

steps.title: King Safety
steps.text: Two pieces out on each side is a good moment to castle. Castling whisks the king into a corner and connects your rooks. Do it before launching attacks.
